title: PNA Toolkit evaluate-report artifact
description: Machine-comparable form of the report produced by the skill's evaluate flow (pna-toolkit/SKILL.md § Evaluate flow). Keyed by AC ID so two runs on the same candidate can be diffed to show exactly which ACs changed status. The human-readable report is a rendering (a view) over an instance of this schema; this artifact is the source of truth. This is NOT a PNA interface contract — it realizes no AC and intentionally lives in tools/, not contracts/ (the lint-spec-ids check requires every file in contracts/ to name an AC it realizes; this one realizes none). It is the typed output of the toolkit's own evaluative tooling. Deterministic checks in tools/ (for example the egress lint) emit evidence entries with source=deterministic that an LLM evaluator folds into the relevant AC finding alongside its own source=llm evidence; source=human covers maintainer or reviewer judgement. Goals 1-4 (see spec/PNA_Spec.md § Goals) are the load-bearing user-facing concerns and drive the summary. A report is emitted in one of two output modes (docs/conformance-scope-and-lifecycle.md § The utility line): membership (Mode 1 — a PNA candidate, verdict by posture) or goal-impact (Mode 2 — an adjacent app that touches the personal-network root without claiming to be a PNA; the AC findings are the instrument, summary.goal_impacts is the verdict, and the posture is not-a-pna). Mode 3 (no nexus, out of scope) emits no report.

report_schema_version.description: Version of THIS report schema (not the PNA Spec version the candidate was evaluated against — that is candidate.pna_spec_version). Lets a diff tool refuse to compare incompatible report shapes. 0.2 is strictly additive over 0.1: it adds candidate.classification (the evaluate flow's Step-0 categorization), summary.goal_impacts (the Mode-2 per-Goal read), and the not-a-pna posture. Every valid 0.1 instance remains valid; a 0.2 instance must carry candidate.classification.

generated_at.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp the emitter stamps when the report was produced. Informational; not used for diffing.

generated_by.description: Optional free-text identity of the evaluator that produced this report (agent/model/tool). Informational.

candidate.description: What was evaluated. Enough to know which design + revision this report describes, so two reports over the same candidate can be diffed meaningfully.

commit.description: Commit SHA (or other revision id) of the source that was read, if known. Two reports at different commits are expected to differ; two at the same commit should not.

pna_spec_version.description: The PNA Spec version the candidate was evaluated against, e.g. '0.1'.

picks_source.description: Whether axis_picks came from the candidate's own Architecture document (declared) or were inferred from the source by the evaluator (inferred). Per SKILL.md § Evaluate flow, both are valid inputs.

axis_picks.description: The candidate's flavor: axis -> pick. Determines which flavor-derived ACs apply (see spec/axes.md). Keys are axis names (e.g. 'distribution', 'storage'); values are pick identifiers (e.g. 'web-bundle-with-magic-link').

summary.description: Top-of-report posture. Per SKILL.md, anything compromising Goals 1-4 leads here.

posture.description: Reports PNA membership (the pna-active bit, exceptions.md § Concept) together with conformance; exception *handling* lives solely in the per-EX findings and is never rolled up here. conformant = a PNA right now (no exception active) AND no non-conformances AND nothing left undetermined. not-pna-active = an exception is active (pna-active = false), so the app is not a PNA right now — a cleanly-handled EX-CLOUD-LLM app reads this, never conformant. non-conformant = at least one Goal 1-4 AC is non-conformant. mixed = some non-conformances but none on Goals 1-4, or non-conformances coexisting with conformant findings. indeterminate = too many unable-to-determine findings to take a posture. not-a-pna = the Mode-2 posture: the candidate never claimed PNA membership (classification.mode = goal-impact), so a membership verdict would be noise — the verdict is summary.goal_impacts, and the classification block says what the app IS. Distinct from not-pna-active, which is a PNA-claimant temporarily out of PNA mode under an active Exception; not-a-pna is an app that was never a claimant at all. The two membership-verdict postures (conformant / non-conformant) are reserved for Mode-1 reports; not-a-pna is required for, and exclusive to, Mode-2 reports.

headline.description: One- or two-sentence prose summary a human reads first. Should name the most concerning non-conformances, Goals 1-4 first.

leading_concerns.description: AC IDs (matching findings[].ac_id) the summary calls out as most concerning, in priority order. Typically the Goal 1-4 non-conformances.

goal_impacts.description: The Mode-2 verdict: the candidate's relationship to each of the four Goals on the fixed scale from docs/conformance-scope-and-lifecycle.md § Mode 2 (protects / neutral / diminishes / mixed / out-of-scope). Exactly one entry per Goal. Required when classification.mode = goal-impact; permitted (informative) on a membership report. Bounded strictly to the Goals — never a general privacy or quality score.

findings.description: One entry per AC the evaluator considered for the candidate's flavor. The diff signal: same candidate.commit + same report_schema_version should produce the same set of (ac_id -> status); a status change between two runs is the regression/improvement signal. In a Mode-2 (goal-impact) report the findings still carry real statuses — they are the instrument that makes the per-Goal read precise and diffable — but a non-conformant entry there is a class-contrast observation feeding goal_impacts, not the failure of a claim; the verdict is summary.goal_impacts.

allOf.$comment: A 0.2 instance must classify the candidate (the Step-0 categorization is recorded as a matter of course).
Mode-2 coupling, forward: a goal-impact report's verdict is the per-Goal read, and its posture must be not-a-pna (a membership verdict on a non-claimant is noise).
Mode-2 coupling, backward: the not-a-pna posture is exclusive to goal-impact reports — a membership (Mode-1) report may not use it.

classification.description: The evaluate flow's Step-0 output (pna-toolkit/SKILL.md § Evaluate flow): what kind of application the candidate is and which output mode the evaluation ran in (the three-mode line in docs/conformance-scope-and-lifecycle.md § The utility line). Recorded as a matter of course, so a report on an adjacent app (a messenger, a mail client, an editor a user keeps contacts in) leads with an honest categorization instead of a membership verdict the candidate never claimed.

application_class.description: What the candidate is, named plainly — e.g. 'end-to-end-encrypted messaging client', 'personal relationship manager', 'text editor'. From its own claims plus its architectural shape.

stores_contact_data.description: Does the candidate hold contact data (spec/PNA_Spec.md § Vocabulary — externally-sourced facts identifying people: names, emails, phones, group rosters)?

contact_data_note.description: Optional one-liner on where/how the contact data lives (e.g. 'local SQLCipher store synced to the vendor's storage service').

stores_relationship_data.description: Does the candidate hold private relationship data (spec/PNA_Spec.md § Vocabulary — user-created memory over contacts: notes, tags, groups, comms history, sensitive communications)?

relationship_data_note.description: Optional one-liner on where/how the relationship data lives.

nexus.description: The candidate's nexus with the personal-network root. pna-candidate = claims to be a PNA or is architecturally shaped like one (a sovereign private layer, local-first, downstream of SaaS). adjacent = does not claim, but stores contact and/or relationship data. none = no personal-network root to protect or diminish.

nexus_source.description: How the nexus was established. claimed = the candidate itself claims PNA membership (an Architecture document, an explicit statement). inferred = the evaluator read it from the source/architecture. user-declared = the evaluation runner supplied it ('it's an editor, but I keep all my contacts in it') — record the declaration verbatim in user_declaration.

user_declaration.description: The runner's verbatim statement establishing nexus. Required when nexus_source = user-declared.

mode.description: Which output mode the evaluation ran in (docs/conformance-scope-and-lifecycle.md § The utility line). membership = Mode 1, a full conformance verdict. goal-impact = Mode 2, per-Goal protects/neutral/diminishes/mixed/out-of-scope with posture not-a-pna. Mode 3 (nexus = none and no user declaration) emits no report — the honest output is 'out of scope', said in prose. A runner may force membership on an adjacent app; record nexus honestly either way.

goal_impact.description: One Goal's read on the fixed Mode-2 scale. The high-value signal is diminishes — the app erodes the user's control of their personal-network root; mixed = genuinely both directions on one Goal (name both facets in the note).

impact.description: protects = actively advances what the Goal protects. neutral = neither helps nor erodes. diminishes = erodes the user's control of their personal-network root on this Goal. mixed = materially both (e.g. an exemplary content-blind transport whose private overlay still syncs off-device). out-of-scope = the Goal's subject matter is absent from this app.

note.description: One or two sentences grounding the read, naming the AC findings it leans on. Required for diminishes and mixed (the reads a user acts on must be self-explaining).

ac_id.description: Stable AC identifier as defined in spec/PNA_Spec.md or spec/axes.md, e.g. AC-1, AC-MCP-A, AC-PRM-C.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the cited source file.

lines.description: Optional line or range within the file, e.g. '120' or '120-145'.

note.description: Optional one-line explanation of what this location shows.

evidence.description: A single piece of support for a finding's status. The layering principle (deterministic + LLM + human) shows up here as the source field.

source.description: deterministic = produced by a tools/ check (e.g. the egress lint); llm = the evaluating model's own architectural reading; human = maintainer/reviewer judgement.

tool.description: For source=deterministic, the producing tool's identifier, e.g. 'egress-lint'. Omitted for llm/human.

detail.description: What this evidence shows, in prose.

citations.description: Optional code locations this specific evidence points at (e.g. the offending call site a deterministic check flagged).

ac_source.description: Where the AC is defined: universal (spec/PNA_Spec.md) or flavor-derived (spec/axes.md, triggered by an axis pick).

status.description: Per SKILL.md § Evaluate flow. conformant: design honors the AC. non-conformant: design violates it. not-applicable: the candidate's flavor doesn't trigger this AC. unable-to-determine: evaluator couldn't decide; flags for human review.

goals.description: Which top-level Goals (1-4) this AC bears on. Used to compute summary.posture and order leading_concerns.

requirement.description: The AC's stated normative requirement (the MUST/SHOULD text it constrains). Required when status=non-conformant so the violation is self-explaining.

rationale.description: The evaluator's reasoning. Required for not-applicable (the reason it doesn't apply) and unable-to-determine (what blocked a decision).

citations.description: Code locations supporting the status. Required (non-empty) for conformant and non-conformant.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence entries (deterministic / llm / human). A deterministic tool's output lands here so deterministic and LLM layers co-locate on one AC.

needs_human_review.description: Defaults true semantically for unable-to-determine. Set explicitly when a conformant/non-conformant call still wants a human second look.
